refined_fr.refined_fr: The NGHE solution shall maintain teleoperation link availability at or above the customer-defined target throughout the operational period.
refined_fr.rationale: Refining the original FR to explicitly state the performance metric (link availability) and operational context ensures clarity and testability.
refined_fr.verification_idea: Measure link uptime statistics over the evaluation period and compare against the customer-defined availability target.
sub_frs: FR1: Monitor teleoperation link status continuously.
FR2: Detect and recover from link failures promptly.
FR3: Maintain network link redundancy to ensure availability.

candidate_dps: DP1: Implement continuous link health monitoring and alerting.
DP2: Deploy automatic failover mechanisms for network links.
DP3: Use redundant communication channels with load balancing.
selected_dp.selected_dp: DP1: Implement continuous link health monitoring and alerting.

potential_sources: Link monitoring tightly coupled with network link hardware and teleoperation control logic.
coupling_check.notes: DP1 logically addresses FR1 and supports FR2 and FR3 indirectly; coupling is manageable within the teleoperation domain.

assumptions_risks: Assumes network infrastructure supports monitoring and failover.
Risk of delayed detection impacting availability if monitoring is not real-time.
selected_dp.recommended_revision: Consider integrating failover mechanisms (DP2) in next decomposition level to reduce risk.

im_boundary.name: Teleoperation
im_boundary.scope: Software and hardware components responsible for teleoperation link management and control.

external_interfaces.name: Network Link
external_interfaces.details: Physical and logical network connection used for teleoperation data exchange.

external_interfaces.name: Telemetry
external_interfaces.details: Status and health data of the teleoperation link sent to NGHE Solution and Safety Services.

external_interfaces.name: Tasking
external_interfaces.details: Commands and operational tasks received from NGHE Solution.

module_containment_scores.notes: High containment as link monitoring and control logically belong here; low diffusion risk.
Partial containment possible but would increase diffusion and complexity.
Low containment; vehicle hardware less suited for link monitoring logic.
Minimal containment; safety services consume alerts but do not manage link.

assigned_im.containment_hypothesis: Centralizing link availability monitoring and control within Teleoperation module minimizes diffusion and improves maintainability.
containment_risks: Potential overload of Teleoperation module if failover mechanisms are also included without modular separation.
Interface dependencies with NGHE Solution and Safety Services must be well-defined to avoid coupling.
assigned_im.recommended_revision: Evaluate modular separation of failover and redundancy functions in subsequent levels to reduce complexity.

fr_dp_gate.reason: Selected DP logically satisfies the refined FR and supports measurable verification.

diffusion_risks: Minimal diffusion risk as DP is contained within Teleoperation module.
Potential risk if failover mechanisms are added without clear modular boundaries.
dp_im_gate.containment_notes: Containment scores support Teleoperation as best IM boundary for selected DP.

fr_im_gate.verification_feasibility: Verification via telemetry data and network link status is feasible within Teleoperation module.
fr_im_gate.allocation_clarity: Clear allocation of link availability responsibility to Teleoperation module.

recommended_revisions: Plan next level decomposition to include failover and redundancy DPs with corresponding IM assignments.
Define interface contracts explicitly between Teleoperation, NGHE Solution, and Safety Services.

decision.justification: While the current decomposition is sound, the high safety criticality and availability target suggest including failover and redundancy explicitly at this level to mitigate risks.
next_level_sub_frs: FR1: Monitor teleoperation link status continuously.
FR2: Detect and recover from link failures promptly.
FR3: Maintain network link redundancy to ensure availability.

revise.why: To explicitly include failover and redundancy requirements in the functional decomposition for completeness and risk mitigation.

assumptions: Network infrastructure supports monitoring and failover.
Telemetry data is reliable and timely.
